title: Genesis NPO observes world Aids Day in KwaNonqaba
bodyText:
[{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS -  On 1 December, World Aids Day, Genesis Community Projects NPO hosted an event for its Close the Gap Gampaign, to spread awareness about HIV/Aids.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - On 1 December, World Aids Day, Genesis Community Projects NPO hosted an event for its Close the Gap Gampaign, to spread awareness about HIV/Aids."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This event took place at the Barcelona Circle in KwaNonqaba. The purpose was to reduce stigma and educate children about HIV/Aids, as well as its prevention and treatment."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"World Aids Day takes place annually to raise awareness and give support to people who are affected by it.The linkage officer, John Phillipus of Genesis, said: \"Our primary goal is to identify and re-engage individuals who aren't on HIV treatment and get them into care.\"","html":"World Aids Day takes place annually to raise awareness and give support to people who are affected by it.
The linkage officer, John Phillipus of Genesis, said: \"Our primary goal is to identify and re-engage individuals who aren't on HIV treatment and get them into care.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The importance of treatment to stop the spread of the virus was also a main topic at this event, with the help of educational pamphlets that were handed out by the Genesis group.","html":"The importance of treatment to stop the spread of the virus was also a main topic at this event, with the help of educational pamphlets that were handed out by the Genesis group."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In attendance were the ACVV staff, KwaNonqaba police officers, the Mossel Bay Municipality, Ikhaya Boxing Club and the children of the area.","html":"In attendance were the ACVV staff, KwaNonqaba police officers, the Mossel Bay Municipality, Ikhaya Boxing Club and the children of the area."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"KwaNonqaba Police Station’s Cynthia Nofemele and Constable Jolene Kietas. Photo: Chelsea Pieterse","html":"KwaNonqaba Police Station’s Cynthia Nofemele and Constable Jolene Kietas. Photo: Chelsea Pieterse"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A mobile clinic did HIV testing and general checks, and some entertainment was organised by ACVV and Genesis, such as dancing, boxing, netball and soccer.","html":"A mobile clinic did HIV testing and general checks, and some entertainment was organised by ACVV and Genesis, such as dancing, boxing, netball and soccer."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Hotdogs, soup, bread and water were handed out to the children. The atmosphere was lively and exciting, and the children had fun as they danced.","html":"Hotdogs, soup, bread and water were handed out to the children. The atmosphere was lively and exciting, and the children had fun as they danced."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Lazola Thole of the Ikhaya Boxing Club holds some demonstrations with some of the young children and teenagers. Photo: Chelsea Pieterse","html":"Lazola Thole of the Ikhaya Boxing Club holds some demonstrations with some of the young children and teenagers. Photo: Chelsea Pieterse"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
